{"key_event":"2005 reforms and Penguin Revolution","reasoning":"Civil society moved back into the strong range as human-rights groups, courts, students, indigenous organizations, and a freer democratic public sphere increasingly constrained state action and eroded military impunity. State power rose slightly because civilian authority, fiscal capacity, judicial reach, and social-policy administration strengthened, though implementation failures such as Transantiago and legitimacy gaps limited the gain.","society_change":0.8,"society_power":7.5,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":8.8}
